summary: Excellent, crystal‑clear source captures a standout Spring‑'88 performance highlighted by a soaring Lay Me Down > Ballad of a Thin Man segue and an epic Other One.
review: The recording is praised as outstanding and remarkably clear, letting listeners hear every nuance of the band’s late‑80s resurgence.  Musically the show shines from start to finish – the first set dazzles with rare gems like Lay Me Down into Ballad of a Thin Man, while the second set delivers an intense post‑drums space jam that blossoms into one of the era’s best Other Ones.  Overall it’s a top‑tier Spring ’88 concert captured on a superb source.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 5‑star praise for both performance and sound quality across 7 of 9 reviews; only two low ratings relate to availability, not the music or recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly describe the source as outstanding, crisp and very fine with no noted drop‑outs or distortion.

standout_songs: Lay Me Down > Ballad of a Thin Man
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is above‑average with rare, powerful Dylan covers; second set builds smoothly, highlighted by an intense post‑drums space jam that leads into a deep, exploratory Other One.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Late‑80s resurgence noted; raspier vocals add character and his guitar work is praised as brilliant throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drums space jam described as seriously intense and a major highlight of the show.
